title: cbrt_meta Function Verification
text: : Example used cbRt_meta (incorrect casing) : Example uses cbrt_meta (correct function name) Added: @return documentation

title: cbrt_meta Function Verification
text: New: Check token empty provide helpful error message : fail cryptic HTTP error token provided : Clear error: “API token found. Please provide token set EVDS_TOKEN environment variable.”

title: cbrt_meta Function Verification
text: Removed: Unused join categories data (joined never used output) Simplified: Function now clearer, direct logic

title: cbrt_meta Function Verification
code: cbrt_meta(token)     ↓ 1. Check token (get from env if not provided)     ↓ 2. Validate token (error if empty)     ↓ 3. Get all categories from API     ↓ 4. Get all data groups from API     ↓ 5. For each data group, get series info     ↓ 6. Combine all series info into single data frame     ↓ 7. Return metadata (47,370 rows × 28 columns)

title: cbrt_meta Function Verification
code: library(cbRt)  # With token from environment metadata <- cbrt_meta()  # With explicit token metadata <- cbrt_meta(token = "your_api_key")

title: cbrt_meta Function Verification
code: library(dplyr)  metadata %>%   filter(grepl("exchange|currency", SERIE_NAME_ENG, ignore.case = TRUE)) %>%   select(SERIE_CODE, SERIE_NAME_ENG, FREQUENCY_STR, START_DATE, END_DATE)

title: cbrt_meta Function Verification
text: Execution time: ~10-30 seconds (depends API response time) Network calls: ~100+ API requests (one per data group) Data size: ~47,370 series 28 metadata fields Memory: Moderate (result ~2-3 MB)

title: cbrt_meta Function Verification
text: Cache Results: applications need metadata frequently, consider caching result avoid repeated API calls Subset Early: need specific categories, consider using lower-level helper functions directly Error Handling: using production, wrap calls tryCatch() handle network errors gracefully

title: cbrt_meta Function Verification
text: get_categories_info(token) - Get category information get_groups_info(token, category_id) - Get data groups get_series_info(token, code) - Get series specific data group cbrt_get() - Retrieve actual time series data

what: Getting a DOI for cbRt via Zenodo
title: Getting a DOI for cbRt via Zenodo
text: guide walks getting DOI cbRt package using Zenodo’s GitHub integration.

title: Getting a DOI for cbRt via Zenodo
text: GitHub repository: https://github.com/emraher/cbRt CITATION.cff file (updated) inst/CITATION file (created) .zenodo.json file (created)

title: Getting a DOI for cbRt via Zenodo
text: Go Zenodo (use production Zenodo, sandbox) Click “Log ” top right Choose “Log GitHub” Authorize Zenodo access GitHub account logged , click username (top right) → “GitHub” ’ll see list repositories Find emraher/cbRt flip switch

title: Getting a DOI for cbRt via Zenodo
text: creating release, commit changes made: Now create tagged release: GitHub: Go https://github.com/emraher/cbRt/releases Click “Draft new release” Choose tag: v0.3.0 (tag just pushed) Release title: cbRt v0.3.0 Description (example): Click “Publish release”
code: git add CITATION.cff inst/CITATION .zenodo.json .Rbuildignore git commit -m "Add Zenodo integration and citation files" git push origin main # Create and push the v0.3.0 tag git tag -a v0.3.0 -m "Release version 0.3.0 - EVDS API v3 support" git push origin v0.3.0 ## cbRt v0.3.0  First archived release with DOI via Zenodo.  ### Major Changes  - Updated to EVDS API v3 with automatic chunking for 150 observation limit - Hybrid frequency detection with metadata fallback - Enhanced package infrastructure following modern R package standards  See NEWS.md for full changelog.

title: Getting a DOI for cbRt via Zenodo
text: Within minutes: Zenodo detect new GitHub release Archive repository snapshot Version-specific DOI (v0.3.0 specifically) Concept DOI (always points latest version)

title: Getting a DOI for cbRt via Zenodo
text: Go https://zenodo.org/ Click username → “Uploads” Find cbRt entry ’ll see DOIs listed DOI format something like: - Concept DOI: 10.5281/zenodo.XXXXXXX - Version DOI: 10.5281/zenodo.XXXXXXX+1

what: 5. Update Your Files with the DOI
title: Getting a DOI for cbRt via Zenodo
text: DOIs, update files:

previous_headings: Step-by-Step Instructions > 5. Update Your Files with the DOI
title: Getting a DOI for cbRt via Zenodo
text: Add lines date-released field:
code: doi: 10.5281/zenodo.XXXXXXX  # Use the CONCEPT DOI here

previous_headings: Step-by-Step Instructions > 5. Update Your Files with the DOI
title: Getting a DOI for cbRt via Zenodo
text: Add doi field bibentry:
code: bibentry(   bibtype  = "Manual",   title    = "cbRt: R Interface to EDDS of CBRT",   author   = person("Emrah", "Er", email = "eer@eremrah.com",                     comment = c(ORCID = "0000-0001-9909-7479")),   year     = 2026,   note     = "R package version 0.3.0",   url      = "https://eremrah.com/cbRt/",   doi      = "10.5281/zenodo.XXXXXXX",  # Concept DOI   textVersion = paste(     "Er, E. (2026).",     "cbRt: R Interface to EDDS of CBRT.",     "R package version 0.3.0.",     "https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.XXXXXXX"   ) )

previous_headings: Step-by-Step Instructions > 5. Update Your Files with the DOI
title: Getting a DOI for cbRt via Zenodo
text: Add DOI badge existing badges: regenerate README.md:
code: [![DOI](https://zenodo.org/badge/DOI/10.5281/zenodo.XXXXXXX.svg)](https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.XXXXXXX) rmarkdown::render("README.Rmd")

title: Getting a DOI for cbRt via Zenodo
code: git add CITATION.cff inst/CITATION README.Rmd README.md git commit -m "Add Zenodo DOI to citation files and README" git push origin main

title: Getting a DOI for cbRt via Zenodo
text: future releases (e.g., v0.4.0): Update version DESCRIPTION, CITATION.cff, NEWS.md Commit changes Create new git tag: git tag -v0.4.0 -m "Release version 0.4.0" Push tag: git push origin v0.4.0 Create GitHub release () Zenodo automatically create NEW version-specific DOI concept DOI remains (always points latest)

title: Getting a DOI for cbRt via Zenodo
text: Instruct users cite follows:

what: For general citation (recommended for most papers):
title: Getting a DOI for cbRt via Zenodo
text: Use concept DOI - always resolves latest version.
code: Er, E. (2026). cbRt: R Interface to EDDS of CBRT. R package version 0.3.0. https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.XXXXXXX

title: Getting a DOI for cbRt via Zenodo
text: Use version-specific DOI:
code: Er, E. (2026). cbRt: R Interface to EDDS of CBRT (Version 0.3.0). Zenodo. https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.XXXXXXX+1

title: Getting a DOI for cbRt via Zenodo
text: creating release, can test Zenodo Sandbox: Go https://sandbox.zenodo.org/ Follow steps Create test release tag like v0.3.0-test Verify DOI gets minted Delete sandbox record test release Proceed production Zenodo

title: Getting a DOI for cbRt via Zenodo
text: Zenodo didn’t archive release: - Check repository toggle Zenodo’s GitHub settings - Make sure created “Release” GitHub, just tag - Wait 5-10 minutes; sometimes ’s delay want delete DOI: - delete published DOIs (’re permanent identifiers) - can mark “newer version available” Zenodo made mistake .zenodo.json: - can edit metadata Zenodo’s website publication - Future releases use updated .zenodo.json

title: Getting a DOI for cbRt via Zenodo

title: Getting Started with cbRt
text: cbRt package provides R interface Electronic Data Delivery System (EVDS) Central Bank Republic Turkey (CBRT). vignette guide basic usage package.

title: Getting Started with cbRt
text: can install cbRt GitHub:
code: # Install from GitHub # install.packages("devtools") devtools::install_github("emraher/cbRt")

title: Getting Started with cbRt
text: use CBRT EVDS API, need API token. Follow steps: Visit https://evds3.tcmb.gov.tr/ Create account log Navigate profile generate API key

title: Getting Started with cbRt
text: can store token .Renviron file persistent access: Alternatively, set current session:
code: # Add this line to your .Renviron file # You can edit it with: usethis::edit_r_environ() EVDS_TOKEN = "your_api_key_here" Sys.setenv(EVDS_TOKEN = "your_api_key_here")

title: Getting Started with cbRt
code: library(cbRt) library(dplyr) library(ggplot2)

what: Retrieving Data for a Single Series
title: Getting Started with cbRt
text: Let’s retrieve USD/TRY exchange rate data: data returned tibble Date column series values.
code: # Get USD/TRY exchange rate (buying rate) usd_try <- cbrt_get(   series = "TP.DK.USD.A",   start_date = "01-01-2020",   end_date = "01-01-2024",   token = Sys.getenv("EVDS_TOKEN") )  # View the data head(usd_try)

title: Getting Started with cbRt
text: can retrieve multiple series . package automatically handle different frequencies:
code: # Get multiple exchange rates exchange_rates <- cbrt_get(   series = c("TP.DK.USD.A", "TP.DK.EUR.A", "TP.DK.GBP.A"),   start_date = "01-01-2023",   end_date = "01-01-2024",   token = Sys.getenv("EVDS_TOKEN") )  # View the data structure head(exchange_rates)

title: Getting Started with cbRt
text: EVDS API supports various data transformations formulas. Available formulas: 0: Level (default) 1: Percentage change 2: Difference 3: Year--year Percent Change 4: Year--year Differences 5: Percentage Change Compared End--Previous Year 6: Difference Compared End--Previous Year 7: Moving Average 8: Moving Sum

title: Getting Started with cbRt
code: # Get year-to-year percent change usd_try_yoy <- cbrt_get(   series = "TP.DK.USD.A",   start_date = "01-01-2020",   end_date = "01-01-2024",   formulas = 3,  # Year-to-year percent change   token = Sys.getenv("EVDS_TOKEN") )  head(usd_try_yoy)

title: Getting Started with cbRt
code: # Apply different formulas to different series data <- cbrt_get(   series = c("TP.DK.USD.A", "TP.DK.EUR.A"),   start_date = "01-01-2023",   end_date = "01-01-2024",   formulas = c(1, 3),  # Percentage change for USD, YoY for EUR   token = Sys.getenv("EVDS_TOKEN") )

title: Getting Started with cbRt
text: cbrt_meta() function retrieves metadata available series:
code: # Get all available series metadata metadata <- cbrt_meta(token = Sys.getenv("EVDS_TOKEN"))  # Explore the structure glimpse(metadata)  # Search for specific series metadata %>%   filter(grepl("exchange", SERIE_NAME_ENG, ignore.case = TRUE)) %>%   select(SERIE_CODE, SERIE_NAME_ENG, FREQUENCY_STR, START_DATE, END_DATE) %>%   head()

title: Getting Started with cbRt
text: EVDS API v3 limit 150 observations per request. cbRt package automatically handles : Detecting frequency series Splitting large date ranges chunks Fetching data multiple requests Combining results transparently

title: Getting Started with cbRt
code: # This will automatically be chunked usd_try_long <- cbrt_get(   series = "TP.DK.USD.A",   start_date = "01-01-2015",   end_date = "01-01-2024",   token = Sys.getenv("EVDS_TOKEN") )  # The package will display messages like: # "Series TP.DK.USD.A: Fetching data in 5 chunks to handle 150 observation limit."  # Check the number of observations nrow(usd_try_long)

title: Getting Started with cbRt
code: # Plot USD/TRY exchange rate usd_try %>%   ggplot(aes(x = Date, y = TP.DK.USD.A)) +   geom_line(color = "#33337E", linewidth = 1) +   labs(     title = "USD/TRY Exchange Rate",     subtitle = "Central Bank of Turkey (Buying Rate)",     x = "Date",     y = "USD/TRY",     caption = "Source: CBRT EVDS"   ) +   theme_minimal()

title: Getting Started with cbRt
text: default, cbrt_get() returns tibble, can specify formats:
code: # As tibble (default) data_tibble <- cbrt_get(   series = "TP.DK.USD.A",   start_date = "01-01-2023",   end_date = "01-01-2024",   as = "tibble",   token = Sys.getenv("EVDS_TOKEN") )  # As tsibble (for time series analysis) data_tsibble <- cbrt_get(   series = "TP.DK.USD.A",   start_date = "01-01-2023",   end_date = "01-01-2024",   as = "tsibble",   token = Sys.getenv("EVDS_TOKEN") )  # As data.frame data_df <- cbrt_get(   series = "TP.DK.USD.A",   start_date = "01-01-2023",   end_date = "01-01-2024",   as = "data.frame",   token = Sys.getenv("EVDS_TOKEN") )  # As data.table data_dt <- cbrt_get(   series = "TP.DK.USD.A",   start_date = "01-01-2023",   end_date = "01-01-2024",   as = "data.table",   token = Sys.getenv("EVDS_TOKEN") )

title: Getting Started with cbRt
text: CBRT data sometimes includes “ND” (Data) values. default, converted NA:
code: # ND values are automatically converted to NA (default: nd = TRUE) data_with_na <- cbrt_get(   series = "TP.DK.USD.A",   start_date = "01-01-2023",   end_date = "01-01-2024",   nd = TRUE,  # Convert ND to NA (default)   token = Sys.getenv("EVDS_TOKEN") )  # Keep ND as character strings data_with_nd <- cbrt_get(   series = "TP.DK.USD.A",   start_date = "01-01-2023",   end_date = "01-01-2024",   nd = FALSE,  # Keep ND as-is   token = Sys.getenv("EVDS_TOKEN") )

title: Getting Started with cbRt
text: Store API token securely: Use .Renviron rather hardcoding scripts Check metadata first: Use cbrt_meta() find correct series codes mindful date ranges: large date ranges result multiple API calls Use appropriate formulas: Apply transformations API level possible Handle missing data: Always check NA values analysis

title: Getting Started with cbRt
text: information: Package documentation: ?cbrt_get, ?cbrt_meta Report issues: https://github.com/emraher/cbRt/issues CBRT EVDS website: https://evds3.tcmb.gov.tr/

title: Getting Started with cbRt
text: software way affiliated, endorsed, approved Central Bank Republic Turkey (CBRT). comes absolutely warranty.

title: Authors and Citation
text: Emrah Er. Author, maintainer.

title: Authors and Citation
text: Er, E. (2026). cbRt: R Interface EDDS CBRT. R package version 0.3.0. https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.18381733
code: @Manual{,   title = {cbRt: R Interface to EDDS of CBRT},   author = {Emrah Er},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 0.3.0},   url = {https://eremrah.com/cbRt/},   doi = {10.5281/zenodo.18381733}, }

title: cbRt
text: cbRt provides R interface Electronic Data Delivery System (EVDS) Central Bank Republic Turkey (CBRT). package enables easy access economic data including exchange rates, interest rates, inflation indicators. Features: Full EVDS API v3 support automatic handling 150 observation limit Intelligent chunking - automatically splits large date ranges multiple requests Hybrid frequency detection - detects series frequency metadata fallback Multiple output formats - tibble, tsibble, data.frame, data.table Formula transformations - apply calculations API level Clean data handling - automatic conversion ND values NA

title: cbRt
text: Install development version GitHub:
code: # install.packages("devtools") devtools::install_github("emraher/cbRt")

title: cbRt
text: need API token CBRT EVDS system: Visit https://evds3.tcmb.gov.tr/ Create account generate API key Store token securely .Renviron file: Alternatively, set current session:
code: # Edit your .Renviron file usethis::edit_r_environ()  # Add this line: EVDS_TOKEN = "your_api_key_here" Sys.setenv(EVDS_TOKEN = "your_api_key_here")

title: cbRt
code: library(cbRt)  # Get USD/TRY exchange rate usd_try <- cbrt_get(   series = "TP.DK.USD.A",   start_date = "01-01-2020",   end_date = "01-01-2024",   token = Sys.getenv("EVDS_TOKEN") ) #> Series TP.DK.USD.A: Fetching data in 10 chunks to handle 150 observation limit.  head(usd_try) #> # A tibble: 6 × 2 #>   Date       TP_DK_USD_A #>   <date>           <dbl> #> 1 2020-01-01       NA    #> 2 2020-01-02        5.94 #> 3 2020-01-03        5.95 #> 4 2020-01-04       NA    #> 5 2020-01-05       NA    #> 6 2020-01-06        5.96

title: cbRt
text: Retrieve multiple series - package handles different frequencies automatically:
code: # Get multiple exchange rates exchange_rates <- cbrt_get(   series = c("TP.DK.USD.A", "TP.DK.EUR.A", "TP.DK.GBP.A"),   start_date = "01-01-2023",   end_date = "01-01-2024",   token = Sys.getenv("EVDS_TOKEN") ) #> Series TP.DK.USD.A: Fetching data in 3 chunks to handle 150 observation limit. #> Series TP.DK.EUR.A: Fetching data in 3 chunks to handle 150 observation limit. #> Series TP.DK.GBP.A: Fetching data in 3 chunks to handle 150 observation limit.  head(exchange_rates) #> # A tibble: 6 × 4 #>   Date       TP_DK_EUR_A TP_DK_GBP_A TP_DK_USD_A #>   <date>           <dbl>       <dbl>       <dbl> #> 1 2023-01-01        NA          NA          NA   #> 2 2023-01-02        19.9        22.5        18.7 #> 3 2023-01-03        20.0        22.5        18.7 #> 4 2023-01-04        19.8        22.3        18.7 #> 5 2023-01-05        19.8        22.5        18.7 #> 6 2023-01-06        19.9        22.5        18.7

title: cbRt
text: Available formulas: 0 (level), 1 (percentage change), 2 (difference), 3 (year--year percent change), 4 (year--year differences), 5 (percentage change vs. end--previous year), 6 (difference vs. end--previous year), 7 (moving average), 8 (moving sum).
code: # Get year-over-year percent change usd_try_yoy <- cbrt_get(   series = "TP.DK.USD.A",   start_date = "01-01-2020",   end_date = "01-01-2024",   formulas = 3,  # Year-to-year percent change   token = Sys.getenv("EVDS_TOKEN") ) #> Series TP.DK.USD.A: Fetching data in 10 chunks to handle 150 observation limit.  head(usd_try_yoy) #> # A tibble: 6 × 2 #>   Date       `TP_DK_USD_A-3` #>   <date>               <dbl> #> 1 2020-01-01            NA   #> 2 2020-01-02            12.5 #> 3 2020-01-03            11.6 #> 4 2020-01-04            NA   #> 5 2020-01-05            NA   #> 6 2020-01-06            NA

title: cbRt
text: Use cbrt_meta() discover available data series:
code: library(dplyr)  # Get all available series metadata metadata <- cbrt_meta(token = Sys.getenv("EVDS_TOKEN"))  # Search for exchange rate series metadata %>%   filter(grepl("exchange", SERIE_NAME_ENG, ignore.case = TRUE)) %>%   select(SERIE_CODE, SERIE_NAME_ENG, FREQUENCY_STR, START_DATE, END_DATE) %>%   head()

title: cbRt
text: package handles EVDS API v3 observation limit automatically:

title: cbRt
text: large date ranges, package: Detects series frequency API metadata Splits date range chunks ≤150 observations Fetches data multiple requests Combines results transparently
code: # This automatically chunks the request usd_try_long <- cbrt_get(   series = "TP.DK.USD.A",   start_date = "01-01-2015",  # 9+ years of daily data   end_date = "01-01-2024",   token = Sys.getenv("EVDS_TOKEN") ) #> Series TP.DK.USD.A: Fetching data in 22 chunks to handle 150 observation limit.  # You'll see messages like: # "Series TP.DK.USD.A: Fetching data in 5 chunks to handle 150 observation limit."  nrow(usd_try_long)  # Returns all data, not limited to 150 #> [1] 3288

title: cbRt
text: Series frequencies detected metadata API. detection fails, defaults daily frequency. Supported frequencies: daily, business days, weekly, bi-monthly, monthly, quarterly, semi-annual, yearly.

title: cbRt
text: Choose preferred output format:
code: # Tibble (default) - for dplyr workflows data_tibble <- cbrt_get(series, start_date, end_date, as = "tibble")  # Tsibble - for time series analysis with fable/feasts data_tsibble <- cbrt_get(series, start_date, end_date, as = "tsibble")  # data.frame - base R compatibility data_df <- cbrt_get(series, start_date, end_date, as = "data.frame")  # data.table - for data.table workflows data_dt <- cbrt_get(series, start_date, end_date, as = "data.table")

what: cbRt: R Interface to EDDS of CBRT — cbRt-package
title: cbRt: R Interface to EDDS of CBRT — cbRt-package
text: R interface Electronic Data Delivery System (EVDS) Central Bank Republic Turkey (CBRT). Provides functions retrieve economic data metadata EVDS API v3, automatic handling 150 observation limit intelligent chunking based series frequency detection.

title: cbRt: R Interface to EDDS of CBRT — cbRt-package
text: Maintainer: Emrah Er eer@eremrah.com (ORCID)

title: _PACKAGE cbRt — cbRt
text: R Interface CBRT EDDS

title: _PACKAGE cbRt — cbRt
text: Electronic Data Delivery System (EDDS) dynamic interactive system presents statistical time series data produced Central Bank Republic Turkey (CBRT) /data produced institutions compiled CBRT. Datasets can accessed cbRt functions.

what: Get the series from CBRT — cbrt_get
title: Get the series from CBRT — cbrt_get
text: Get series CBRT

title: Get the series from CBRT — cbrt_get
code: cbrt_get(   series,   start_date,   end_date,   formulas = NULL,   token = NULL,   nd = TRUE,   as = c("tibble", "tsibble", "data.frame", "data.table") )

title: Get the series from CBRT — cbrt_get
text: series Series Code series argument can obtained CBRT webpage. Search CBRT webpage order find series code use series argument. serial codes displayed. one series selected, takes parameters number series, serial codes separated "-" sign. start_date Start date start_date argument series start date dd-mm-yyyy format. order display frequency desired series, first day corresponding frequency must stated start date dd-mm-yyyy format. end_date End date end_date argument series end date dd-mm-yyyy format. formulas Formulas applied series formulas argument formula applied series. Available formulas ; 0: Level 1: Percentage change 2: Difference 3: Year--year Percent Change 4: Year--year Differences 5: Percentage Change Compared End--Previous Year 6: Difference Compared End--Previous Year 7: Moving Average 8: Moving Sum one series selected, takes parameters number series, serial codes separated "-" sign. parameter entered user, level formula parameter applied relevant series. token API key token argument required API key. See https://evds3.tcmb.gov.tr/ instructions obtain API key. nd Convert ND values NA nd argument TRUE FALSE argument. Data retrieved sometimes includes ND terms. nd set TRUE, NDs converted NAs. Type data return Argument can set either tibble, tsibble, data.frame, data.table obtain different types data classes. tibble default output class.

title: Get the series from CBRT — cbrt_get
text: data frame (tibble, tsibble, data.frame, data.table)

title: Get the series from CBRT — cbrt_get
text: CBRT EVDS API v3 150 observation limit per request. function automatically handles limit : Detecting series frequency metadata (fallback daily) Splitting large date ranges chunks max 150 observations Fetching data multiple requests necessary Combining chunks transparently user large date ranges high-frequency data (daily, business days), data fetched multiple chunks. Progress messages inform chunking occurs. Consider using aggregation formulas narrower date ranges better performance.

title: Get the series from CBRT — cbrt_get
code: # Download the given series for the given dates if (FALSE) cbrt_get(series = "TP.DK.USD.A",                   start_date = "01-01-2017",                   end_date = "01-01-2018",                   token = APIkey) # \dontrun{}

title: Tidy eval helpers — tidyeval
text: functions provide tidy eval-compatible ways capture symbols (sym(), syms(), ensym()), expressions (expr(), exprs(), enexpr()), quosures (quo(), quos(), enquo()). learn tidy eval use tools, read http://rlang.tidyverse.org/articles/tidy-evaluation.html

title: cbRt 0.3.0
text: BREAKING: Updated EVDS API v3 - package now uses new EVDS API v3 endpoint (https://evds3.tcmb.gov.tr/igmevdsms-dis/). previous API v2 endpoints updated.

title: cbRt 0.3.0
text: Automatic chunking large date ranges - new EVDS API v3 150 observation limit per request. package now automatically: Detects series frequency metadata (fallback daily frequency) Splits large date ranges chunks maximum 150 observations Fetches data multiple requests necessary Combines chunks transparently user Provides informative messages chunking occurs Hybrid frequency detection - Added get_series_frequency() helper function attempts retrieve series frequency metadata API, falling back conservative daily frequency detection fails. Smart date chunking - Added calculate_date_chunks() helper function intelligently splits date ranges based series frequency (daily, business days, weekly, bi-monthly, monthly, quarterly, semi-annual, yearly).

title: cbRt 0.3.0
text: Updated documentation reflect new API v3 endpoints Added comprehensive documentation 150 observation limit automatic chunking behavior Updated README new API URLs references Added comprehensive “Getting Started” vignette practical examples Enhanced pkgdown website configuration eerdown template Added package-level documentation (?cbRt-package)

title: cbRt 0.3.0
text: Updated modern R package standards following rpkg-template Added ORCID identifier author DESCRIPTION Configured package use eerdown custom pkgdown template Enhanced GitHub Actions workflows (R-CMD-check, test-coverage, pkgdown) Added community files: CONTRIBUTING.md, PR template, issue templates Added dependabot configuration automated dependency updates Added .lintr configuration code quality checks Added CITATION.cff modern citation format Updated .Rbuildignore .gitignore template standards

title: cbRt 0.3.0
text: update maintains backward compatibility package function level - function signatures remain Users may notice slightly slower performance large date ranges due multiple API requests chunking happens transparently; changes user code required
